chunks.page_summary: This document describes the integration of a HiveMQ Cloud-based MQTT Broker with Rheonics Sensor Module Electronics (SME) for remote monitoring, detailing HiveMQ Cloud features and initial account setup.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

HiveMQ Broker Integration with Rheonics

Modified on: Fri, 5 Sep, 2025 at 4:03 PM

What is the purpose of this article?
This guide explains how to set up a HiveMQ Cloud-based MQTT Broker with the goal of enabling remote monitoring of Rheonics sensor data via MQTT. The account setup and broker (

TABLE OF CONTENTS
* 1. What is HiveMQ?
* 2. Setting up a HiveMQ MQTT Broker
  * 2.1. Creating a HiveMQ Cloud Account
  * 2.2. Cluster management
  * 2.3. Setting User Credentials
* 3. HiveMQ MQTT Broker Information for Node-RED
* 4. Resources

1. What is HiveMQ?
HiveMQ is a data streaming platform based on the MQTT publish-subscribe architecture. The product used from HiveMQ in this article is the HiveMQ Cloud, which offers different payment plans depending on the application. However, the version used in this article is HiveMQ Cloud – Serverless, which provides a free, cloud-hosted MQTT broker with the following features:

| Plan          | Serverless                   |
|---------------|------------------------------|
| Type          | Shared Broker                |
| Pricing       | Free                         |
| Connections   | 100 max                      |
| Traffic Limit | 10 GB data traffic per month |

With this broker, users can publish and subscribe to topics over the internet, making it ideal for testing and remote monitoring of Rheonics Sensor Module Electronics (SME) with Node-RED.

Figure 1: Diagram of HiveMQ Broker implementation for remote monitoring

2. Setting up a HiveMQ MQTT Broker

2.1. Creating a HiveMQ Cloud Account
1. Go to Get Started with HiveMQ (https://www.hivemq.com/company/get-hivemq/).
2. Choose the HiveMQ Cloud option
key_facts: The document aims to explain how to set up a HiveMQ Cloud-based MQTT Broker for remote monitoring of Rheonics sensor data.
HiveMQ is a data streaming platform based on the MQTT publish-subscribe architecture.
The article specifically uses HiveMQ Cloud – Serverless, which provides a free, cloud-hosted MQTT broker.
The HiveMQ Cloud Serverless plan offers a shared broker type, free pricing, a maximum of 100 connections, and a traffic limit of 10 GB data traffic per month.
The broker allows users to publish and subscribe to topics over the internet.
The integration is ideal for testing and remote monitoring of Rheonics Sensor Module Electronics (SME) using Node-RED.

key_facts: HiveMQ Cloud is a free Cloud MQTT Broker that allows connection for up to 100 devices.
HiveMQ Cloud is a fully-managed service designed for IoT messaging needs, offering reliability and security.
Users can start their IoT journey for free with HiveMQ Cloud without needing a credit card.
HiveMQ Cloud supports seamless data integration with third-party services.
HiveMQ Cloud provides complete support for the MQTT specification for adaptable and efficient IoT solutions.
An MQTT broker cluster in HiveMQ acts as a single broker and is based on multiple nodes to ensure system reliability and availability.
The document provides specific URLs and port numbers for cluster connections, including TLS MQTT and TLS Websocket URLs.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions for setting up user credentials in HiveMQ Access Management and configuring the HiveMQ MQTT broker for Node-RED, detailing necessary connection parameters.
chunks.extracted_text:
Figure 5: Clusters information

2.3. Setting User Credentials

1. Press on Access Management in the menu at the top inside your cluster.

Figure 6: Access Management

2. Create credentials to access the broker

*   Username
*   Password
*   Permission: Publish and Subscribe

Figure 7: Creating Credentials in HiveMQ Access Management

3. After creating a credential, the username and the permission are shown.

Figure 8: Created Credentials in HiveMQ successfully

3. HiveMQ MQTT Broker information for Node-RED

To add a new MQTT broker in Node-RED (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000418666), you need the broker's connection details. In the case of the HiveMQ broker, this information can be easily found in the Cluster Management section on the HiveMQ Cloud website, as shown earlier.

1. On the MQTT broker configuration in Node-RED, fill the spaces with the broker information.

*   Server: URL of the broker
*   Port: Port used by the broker
*   Use TLS: Enable this option because HiveMQ uses encrypted communication
key_facts: User credentials for HiveMQ are configured by pressing 'Access Management' within the cluster menu.
Credentials creation for accessing the broker requires a Username, Password, and 'Publish and Subscribe' permission.
After successful credential creation, the username and assigned permission are displayed.
To add a new MQTT broker in Node-RED, connection details for the HiveMQ broker are required.
HiveMQ broker connection details can be found in the 'Cluster Management' section of the HiveMQ Cloud website.
MQTT broker configuration in Node-RED involves filling in the Server URL, Port, and enabling TLS.
TLS must be enabled for HiveMQ communication because it uses encrypted communication.
diagram_labels: Currently you have not created any credentials. Fill out the following form to create an access credentials pair and limit access to your HiveMQ Cloud MQTT instance. To learn more check out our Security Fundamentals guide.

4. Resources
  * Get Started with HiveMQ (https://www.hivemq.com/company/get-hivemq/)
  * About HiveMQ (https://www.hivemq.com/company/)
  * HiveMQ Pricing (https://www.hivemq.com/pricing/self-service/)
key_facts: This document guides users through configuring an MQTT broker in Node-RED.
The configuration process involves setting up connection properties like server address, port, protocol (MQTT V5), client ID, Keep Alive interval, and session management.
Security credentials, including username and password, need to be entered under the 'Security' tab of the MQTT broker configuration.
The broker configuration is finalized by pressing the 'Add' button.
Rheonics sensor data can be sent to an MQTT broker using Node-RED.
Ignition SCADA can be connected to a HiveMQ broker for visualizing Rheonics sensor data.
